zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne de Lou於1235年出生在英國武斯特斯爾德福德時，她的父親Robert De Lou41歲，母親Anne41歲。她於大約1260年與Sir John le Despenser在英國武斯特斯爾德福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1299年在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾德福德享年64歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾德福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anne de Lou于1235年出生在英国武斯特斯爾德福德时，她的父亲Robert De Lou41岁，母亲Anne41岁。她于大约1260年与Sir John le Despenser在英国武斯特斯爾德福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1299年在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾德福德享年64岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾德福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ching-Yun, Jian, Wei, Chi, Hong, Jing, Jong, Kwan, Ting, Wai Keung, Zheng, Bin.  Vietnamese Son, Cao, Hao, Khanh Thi, Ly, Rung, Tien, Tin.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 婁, a name of one of the 28 Mansions in traditional Chinese astronomy: (i) from the second element of the placename Zhu Lou (邾婁), the name of a state (located in Zouxian in Shandong province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ), also known as the state of Zou (鄒) or Zhu (邾). (ii) from the placename Lou (婁), the name of a fief (located in Zhucheng in Shandong province) in the state of Chu. (iii) from the Pi Lou (匹婁), Yi Lou (伊婁), Yi Na Lou (乙那婁) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient northern China, who changed their original surnames to Lou (婁) during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iv) said to be from the personal name of Li Lou (离婁), a legendary figure, famous for his good eyesight, during the reign of Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樓, meaning ‘tower’ or ‘storey’ in Chinese: (i) from the title of Dong Lou Gong (東樓公, Duke Donglou of Qi), who was granted the state of Qi (杞) (originally located in Henan province, later moved to Shandong province) by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) from the Gai Lou (蓋樓) and He Lou (賀樓) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who adopted the surname 樓 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

variants.name: Lu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Wei, Ming, Jian, Min, Ping, Li, Yi, Feng, Ling, Hung, Minh, Nu, Gang, Ha, Muoi, Cheng, Chi, Hoa, Tan, Cuong, Ha To, Dung, Hai, Lan, Chung, Phong, Tuong, Tam, Chang, Manh, Shen, Tian, Tinh, You, Chong.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 盧, meaning both ‘black’in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Lu (盧), the name of a fief (located in Changqing in Shandong province) granted to Xi, a great-grandson of Jiang Tai Gong or Jiang Ziya (c. 11th century BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound Chinese surname Lu-Pu (盧蒲, also written as 蘆蒲), borne by descendants of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643 BC ). (iii) from the placename of Lu (廬), the name of an ancient state (unknown location) prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). When it was adopted as a surname, the Chinese character 廬 was simplified to 盧. (iv) from the Mo Lu (莫蘆) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 蘆 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ), and later some descendants simplified the Chinese character 蘆 to 盧. (v) from the Tu Fu Lu (吐伏盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 盧 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 蘆, meaning ‘reed’ in Chinese: (i) shortened form of the compound Chinese surname Lu Pu (盧蒲, also written as 蘆蒲), which was borne by descendants of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). (ii) from the Mo Lu (莫蘆) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 蘆 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne de Lou was born in 1235, in Defford, Worcestershire, England, her father, Robert De Lou, was 41 and her mother, Anne, was 41. She married Sir John le Despenser about 1260, in Defford, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1299, in Defford, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Defford, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne de Lou est née en 1235, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Robert De Lou, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Anne, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John le Despenser environ 1260, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1299, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anne de Lou 1235 in Defford, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert De Lou 41 und ihre Mutter Anne 41. Sie hat um 1260 in Defford, Worcestershire, England Sir John le Despenser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb 1299 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Defford, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Defford, Worcestershire, England begraben.

Lei sposato Sir John le Despenser circa 1260, nel Defford, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1299, nel Defford, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Defford, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne de Louが1235年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードで生まれた時，父Robert De Louは41歳，母Anneは41歳であった。約1260年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードでSir John le Despenserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1299年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードで64歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne de Lou(이)가 1235년 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert De Lou(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 대략 1260년 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 Sir John le Despenser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anne de Lou nasceu em 1235, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert De Lou, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Anne, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John le Despenser aproximadamente 1260, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1299, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne de Lou родилась в 1235 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert De Lou было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Anne, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John le Despenser около 1260 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1299 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anne de Lou nació en 1235, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert De Lou, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Anne, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Sir John le Despenser aproximadamente 1260, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1299, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
